mnemonics: Every Sentence Can Produce Nouns And Verbs (Constituency Parsing: Noun Phrases, Verb Phrases, etc.)
keyPoints: Constituency parsing breaks down a sentence into sub-phrases or constituents.
Each constituent, such as a Noun Phrase (NP) or Verb Phrase (VP), serves a specific grammatical role.
The result is often represented as a hierarchical tree structure that shows the syntactic structure of the sentence.
commonMistakes: Confusing Constituency Parsing (phrase structure) with Dependency Parsing (word-to-word relationships).
Assuming the entire sentence is the only constituent, instead of recognizing the nested sub-phrases within it.
